Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine), hypoglycemia, and low HbA1c. Urinalysis reveals marked proteinuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though diabetes control appears tight or overcorrected. Dyslipidemia is present (high total and LDL cholesterol). Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and review of glycemic management.
